title: Proclamation No. 375, s. 1956
text:
MALACAÑANG

RESIDENCE OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 375

RESERVING FOR PROVINCIAL CAPITOL, CIVIC CENTER HOSPITAL, AND HIGH SCHOOL SITES PURPOSES A CERTAIN PARCEL OF THE PUBLIC DOMAIN SITUATED IN THE CITY OF MARAWI (FORMERLY DANSALAN), ISLAND OF MINDANAO

Upon the recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture and Natural Resources and pursuant, to the authority vested in me by law, I, Ramon Magsaysay, President of the Philippines, do hereby withdraw from sale or settlement and reserve for provincial capitol, civic center, hospital, and high school sites purposes under the respective administration of the Provincial Government of Lanao, the Director of Hospitals, and the Director of Public Schools, subject to private rights, if any there be, and to future survey and subdivision in accordance with the development plan approved by the National Planning Commission, a certain parcel of land situated in the City of Marawi (formerly Dansalan), island of Mindanao, and more particularly described, to wit:

A parcel of land (the proposed Military Camp site as shown on the attached development plan), situated in the City of Dansalan, island of Mindanao. Bounded on the NW., along lines 1-2, by remaining portion of Camp Keithley Military Reservation (II-5919); along lines 2-4, by remaining portion of Camp Keithley Military Reservation (11-5919); along lines 4-21, by remaining portion of Camp Keithley Military Reservation (residential Bite); and along lines 21-28 and 28-1, by remaining portion of Camp Keithley Military Reservation (11-5919). Beginning at a point marked 1 on the attached development plan of Dansalan City, thence N. 56° 30′ E., 870 meters to point 2; thence N. 80° 25′ E., 580 meters to point 3; thence S. 69° 55′ E., 342 meters to point 4; thence S. 12°. 56′ E., 240 meters to point 5; thence S. 21° 05′ W., 370 meters to point 6; thence S. 68° OV W., 118 meters to point 7; thence S. 14° 06′ E., 25 meters to point 8; thence S. 9″ 10′ W., 100 meters to point 9; thence S. 19° 11′ W., 138.50 meters to point 10; thence S. 10° 28′ W., 60 meters to point 11; thence S. 3° 25′ E., 100 meters to point 12; thence S. 1″: 15′ E., 100 meters to point 13; thence S. 8° 45′ W., 100 meters to point 14; thence S. 39° 10′ W., 70 meters to point 15; thence S. 76° 38′ W., 60 ‘meters to point 16; thence S. ’61° 05′ W., 35 meters to point 17; thence S. 6° 45’ W., 70 meters to point 18; thence S. 29° 05′ W., 110 meters to point 19; thence S. 47° 12′ W., 90 meters to point. 20; thence S. 58° 30′ W., 100 meters to point 21; thence S. 79° 27′ W., 100 meters to point 22; thence S. 75° 47′ W., 115 meters to point 23; thence N. 15° 32′ E., 40 meters to point 24; thence N. 22°, 35′ W., 580 meters to point 25; thence N. 55° 30′ W., 210 meters to point 26; thence N. 66° 20′ W., 140 meters to point 27; thence N. 70° 28′ W., 220 meters to point. 28; thence N. 12° 27′ W., 370 meters to the point of beginning; containing an approximate area of 1,714,514 square meters.

NOTE: All data are approximate and subject to change by future surveys.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the sear of the Republic of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done in the City of Manila, this 26th day of December, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and fifty-six, and of the Independence of the Philippines, the eleventh.

RAMON MAGSAYSAY President of the Philippines

By the President:

FORTUNATO DE LEON Executive Secretary

Source: University Library, Far Eastern University
